g.text: layers flow top → bottom · insertion stack on the left · perception on the right
① Robot cell — sensors & Cartesian controllers
② ROS 2 nodes — thin rclpy wrappers (no transition logic)
③ Canonical seams — pure · host-testable (pytest, no rclpy/ROS/cv2)
④ Primitives — building blocks the seams compose
Controller quirks (carrot lead, below-surface gaps, held R)
live ONLY in the command-path adapters — that is why the SIM
and REAL nodes share one phase model.
Perception handoff (ROS topics, not drawn): socket_detector
Both insertion nodes drive the SAME insertion_intent; SIM vs REAL differs only
in the command-path adapter + safety profile they select.
